_comment: Host-neutral branch-protection config-as-code for twiceshy. scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh translates this to the host's API (GitHub rulesets / Forgejo|Gitea branch_protections). required_checks is a comma-separated list of the exact status-check CONTEXTS the CI publishes (NOT the job IDs) — on Forgejo these are `<workflow name> / <job name> (<event>)`, captured verbatim from a real PR run and all green as of 2026-06-17. required_approvals is 0 by design: the `claude` agent self-merges its own green, fully-gated PRs (it can't approve its own PR, so any non-zero value would block that); set it to 1 if you want a mandatory human approval on every PR. The workflows intentionally have NO paths-ignore so these required contexts always post (a skipped required check hangs a docs-only PR). block_on_outdated_branch is true: with the corpus decoupled into its own data product (ADR-0021 #0083), the engine repo no longer takes ~daily corpus imports, so requiring an up-to-date branch no longer churns open code PRs; require_linear_history + block_force_push still hold. Re-apply with scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh after merge.
required_checks: CI / Lint guard & test (pull_request),security / SAST & dependency audit (twiceshy) (pull_request),security / Secret scan (gitleaks) (pull_request)
